title: Graph Store
description: GraphStore is the abstract port defining the contract for graph persistence operations. Concrete implementations own the physical schema, file layout, and backend-specific storage details.

rules: GraphStore shall be an abstract class with a storagePath constructor parameter.
It defines the contract for all graph persistence operations following hexagonal architecture.
Store must support file nodes carrying textual content and paths.
Store must support symbol, spec, and document nodes.
Store must support persisted relations including dependencies and coverage links.
Store must persist store-level metadata like derivation-freshness and fingerprints.
GraphStore provides explicit open and close methods.
All query and mutation methods must throw StoreNotOpenError if called before open or after close.
GraphStore provides a recreate method for destructive reset operations.
GraphStore provides upsertFile for atomic replacement of file data in a single transaction.
GraphStore provides addRelations for additive relation insertion without removing existing data.
GraphStore provides removeFile to atomically remove the file node, its symbols, and relations.
GraphStore provides query methods for retrieving files, symbols, relations, and specs by their IDs.
GraphStore provides getStatistics returning counts for files, symbols, specs, relations, and metadata.
GraphStore provides upsertSpec and removeSpec for atomic spec data management.
GraphStore provides search APIs for symbols, specs, and documents.
Search results must prioritize exact matches on primary identities.
Results must return match-aware snippets and 1-based line ranges.
GraphStore provides methods to clear all data and retrieve all files or specs.

constraints: All mutations are atomic at the file level.
StoreNotOpenError is thrown on any operation when the store is not open.
The abstract store contract does not prescribe a specific backend or physical schema.

scenarios.name: Operation before open throws
when: getFile is called before opening the store
